description: Date picker built on react-day-picker. Renders a grid of day buttons with month / year navigation. Each day is a real <button>, focus is managed by react-day-picker so arrow keys move through the grid, and an effect in CalendarDayButton syncs DOM focus with the focused modifier.

keyboard.action: Moves focus into the calendar (to the focused day) and back out.
Moves focus to the previous / next day.
Moves focus to the same weekday in the previous / next week.
Moves to the first / last day of the current week.
Jumps one month (Shift+PageUp / Shift+PageDown jumps one year).

focus.notes: Only one day in the grid is a tab stop at a time (roving tabindex). The focused day gets a ring via group-data-[focused=true]. Nav buttons and dropdowns are normal tab stops.

announcements: Each day button announces its date; selected state is read when the Selected modifier is active.
Navigation buttons announce as "Previous month" / "Next month" via react-day-picker defaults.
Weekday headers are read at the top of the grid.
screenReader.notes: Today's date gets a subtle background but no ARIA marker; if your users rely on "today" specifically, pair the calendar with a live region that confirms the current selection.

contrast.text: Day numbers use foreground; muted-foreground on outside / disabled days. Selected days use primary-foreground on primary; all meet WCAG AA
contrast.ui: Focus ring uses ring-ring at 3px with 50% opacity; meets AA as a non-text indicator

motion.reducedMotion: Month transitions are driven by react-day-picker and use CSS animation via the rdp classnames. Consumers that need strict reduced-motion compliance should override those keyframes with a motion-safe media query in their theme.

consumerResponsibilities: Localize the calendar by passing react-day-picker's locale / weekStartsOn props; the component only sets a formatMonthDropdown default.
When used inside a Popover (for a date field), ensure the trigger has an accessible label like "Choose date" and reflects the current value.
For date ranges, pair with a visible preview of the selected range so screen reader users can confirm their selection.
